title: ForgeBrain Content Strategy
description: The structured content strategy produced by the Content Director from a lesson blueprint. Decides HOW a lesson is presented (hook, pacing, visuals, emotional arc) without writing any dialogue. Produced after the lesson stage (see lesson-spec.md) and consumed directly by the Script Generator. See content-director-spec.md for the full decision process this schema supports.

topic_id.description: Matches the corresponding id in curriculum/java-roadmap.json and the topic_id of the lesson blueprint this strategy was built from.

topic_title.description: Matches the corresponding title in curriculum/java-roadmap.json.

hook_type.description: The hook category selected for this reel's opening. See content-director-spec.md Section 5.1 for selection signals.

hook_reason.description: Which specific signal in the lesson (e.g. common_mistakes[0], core_example contrast, topic's interview relevance) drove this hook choice. Must name the signal, not just restate the hook type.

teaching_style.description: The opening posture for this reel. Distinct from the lesson blueprint's own teaching_style — see content-director-spec.md Section 3.

teaching_style_reason.description: Why this specific lesson's content justified this opening posture, referencing the lesson's own teaching_style as a starting signal where relevant.

emotional_goal.description: The feeling this reel is engineered to produce by its final beat, chosen to match the hook.

emotional_goal_reason.description: Why this emotional goal follows from the chosen hook and content.

pacing.description: The overall pacing tier. See content-director-spec.md Section 5.5 for scene-count and duration guidance per tier.

pacing_reason.description: Why this pacing tier fits this lesson's content and hook.

scene_pacing.description: Ordered scene breakdown with approximate durations. May resequence the lesson's step_by_step_explanation for storytelling effect, but must still cover every concept the lesson commits to. Should sum to slightly less than target_duration_seconds.

visual_style.description: The primary visual technique for this reel.

supporting_visuals.description: 0-2 secondary visual cues, supporting the primary visual_style.

visual_style_reason.description: Why this visual technique best serves the lesson's core_example.focus_note or analogy.

code_style.description: How the lesson's already-chosen core_example should be framed. Does not choose a different example than the lesson committed to.

code_style_reason.description: Why this framing fits the lesson's core_example.description.

cta_style.description: The call-to-action category for this reel's closing beat.

cta_reason.description: Why this CTA matches the reel's emotional payoff.

retention_goal.description: A single sentence describing the specific mechanism keeping a viewer watching to the end. Design intent for the Reviewer to check the script against, not a metric.

estimated_watch_time.description: Plain-language prediction, in seconds, of how long an average viewer is expected to stay. Typically at or slightly below target_duration_seconds.

target_duration_seconds.description: Carried from the lesson blueprint's target_duration_seconds unless explicitly overridden.

content_director_version.description: Version of the Content Director process/schema that produced this strategy, for future caching and migration.

generated_at.description: When this content strategy was produced.

based_on_lesson_version.description: The lesson_version of the lesson blueprint this strategy was built from, for traceability.

scene.description: Short description of this beat's content and role (e.g. 'cold-open crash', 'rewind to context').

duration_seconds.description: Approximate duration for this scene. Not frame-accurate — the Script Generator has final say on actual timing.

overall_confidence.description: Coarse self-assessment of how well-grounded this strategy's choices are.

flagged_uncertainties.description: Close calls between strategies, or strategy changes made in response to a prior revision, flagged for reviewer attention.

unresolved_conflicts.description: Reserved for future analytics-informed contradictions (e.g. two strategy signals disagreeing once strategy_performance data exists). Empty until relevant.
